'''Mordagon''' was a celestial body<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> located in the [[Mordagon system/Legends|Mordagon system]], a part of the [[Authala sector/Legends|Authala sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Mordageen]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Bendix Fust]].<ref name="Prima" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mordagon was introduced in [[Prima Games]]' [[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|Official Strategy Guide]] for<ref name="Prima" /> the [[November 19]], [[2002]]<ref name="SWBHdate">{{WebCite|url=http://www.gamefaqs.com/ps2/557901-star-wars-bounty-hunter/data|text=''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'' Release Information for PlayStation 2|work=[[Wikipedia:GameFAQs|GameFAQs]]|archivedate=20160304090346}}</ref> [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''.<ref name="SWBH">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref> The guide was authored by [[David Hodgson]]<ref name="Prima" /> and published on [[November 26]] of the same year.<ref name="Amazon">{{Amazon|url=Bounty-Hunter-Primas-Official-Strategy/dp/0761541640|text=Star Wars Bounty Hunter (Prima's Official Strategy Guide)}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Mordagon [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body, in grid square P-12.<ref name="TEA" />
